purpose: Install one owned desktop shortcut that delegates to the canonical Windows Profile launcher and allocates one unique tmux session per click.

directories.purpose: Shortcut-specific maps, registries, workflows, schema, graph, and synthetic fixtures.
Canonical Windows Profile launcher, installer, manifest, and read-only status surface.
Opt-in validation hooks; never installed implicitly.
Scoped operational procedures and stop conditions.
English operator state, gaps, rollback, and proof ceilings.
Windows and Linux exact-head contract proof.

commands.name: Install desktop shortcut
commands.effect: writes an owned shortcut and installed launcher under the current user's profile

commands.name: Preview installation
commands.proof: resolved paths and delegation contract only

commands.name: Preview next identity
commands.effect: writes local plan evidence outside the repository

commands.name: Focused completeness
commands.proof: tracked files, contracts, plan behavior, and registration

commands.name: Dependency-free contracts
commands.proof: allocation, manifest, workflow, CMD, and registration semantics

knownTraps: A shortcut may not call raw WezTerm or tmux lifecycle logic; it delegates to the canonical AgentSwitchboard launcher.
A second WezTerm window attached to dev is not a new tmux instance.
new-instance sessions are named dev-1, dev-2, and so on; the bare dev identity remains reserved for open-or-activate.
WezTerm must receive start --always-new-process for the new-instance path.
The installer may not overwrite a foreign desktop shortcut with the same name.
Installing the shortcut does not prove WSL, tmux, WezTerm, window visibility, attachment, or operator acceptance.
The open-or-activate launcher path is implemented but live workstation proof remains separate.
proofCeiling: Tracked shortcut installer, canonical new-instance implementation, deterministic allocation, installer readback contracts, and offline validation. Live workstation behavior remains separate proof.
